entities.name: LETTUCE
entities.description: Lactuca sativa L., the crop studied for seed viability and chromosome damage during storage

entities.name: SEED VIABILITY
entities.description: The ability of seeds to germinate and grow, which decreases during storage

entities.name: CHROMOSOME DAMAGE
entities.description: The frequency of aberrant cells and chromosomal aberrations observed during first mitoses

entities.name: STORAGE
entities.description: The process of keeping seeds over time under various environmental conditions

entities.name: MOISTURE CONTENT
entities.description: The percentage of water in seeds, which influences the rate of viability loss and chromosome damage

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The thermal environment during storage affecting the rate of chromosomal aberrations

entities.name: DIPLONTIC SELECTION
entities.description: A process used to reduce the frequency of chromosomal aberrations in aged seeds

entities.name: RECESSIVE MUTATIONS
entities.description: Heritable mutations analyzed in the progenies of aged seeds

entities.name: GENETIC CONSERVATION
entities.description: The practice of preserving seeds, requiring specific moisture content for orthodox seeds

entities.name: REGENERATION STANDARD
entities.description: The threshold of seed viability allowed during storage before the seed stock must be regenerated

relationships.description: Lettuce seeds experience loss of viability during storage
Lettuce seeds show chromosome damage during first mitoses after storage
Storage leads to the loss of seed viability
Storage is associated with an increase in the frequency of chromosome damage
Moisture content affects the proportion of aberrant cells, with lower moisture generally reducing the rate of increase
Moisture content influences the rate of loss of germination/viability
Temperature affects the increase in chromosomal aberrations per unit time
Diplontic selection markedly reduces the frequency of chromosomal aberrations
Genetic conservation of orthodox seeds is recommended at moisture contents of 5% or lower
The regeneration standard is defined by the percentage to which seed viability falls
